meta.reportLabel: Wed - THE CEASEFIRE FAILS and oil RE-FIRES: a 2nd Hormuz tanker is hit overnight and the Houthis threaten a Saudi blockade, so WTI jumps back to ~$84.3 (+2.2%) and Brent ~$91.1. That snaps the whole oil-premium map back on: CAD's tailwind returns, the USD's inflation bid is back with a number (CME July-hike odds JUMPED to ~46.5% from 34% Sunday), and gold rips to ~$4,115 with silver +4.5% to ~$59.7. The day's fresh catalyst is British: UK June CPI ran HOT at 3.6% (core 3.7%), motor-fuel driven - GBP joins NZD as a fuel-CPI hawk. USD/JPY PUNCHES to ~163.2, deep in the strike zone. AUD/NZD SHORT (HIGH); GBP/JPY, CAD/JPY, NZD/JPY, GBP/AUD LONG (MED); USOIL/XAU/XAG UPGRADED to LONG; NZD/CAD flips back to RANGE.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: Yesterday's two-way resolved to the upside: the 10-day ceasefire was not accepted, Iran hit a second Hormuz tanker overnight and the Houthis threatened a naval blockade of Saudi Arabia, so crude re-fired (WTI ~$84.3, Brent ~$91.1). That restores every oil-linked leg - CAD's tailwind, the USD's inflation bid (now with July-hike odds at ~46.5%), and a ripping gold/silver bid. UK June CPI ran hot at 3.6% (core 3.7%) on motor fuels, making GBP the day's mover alongside a still-hawkish NZD; JPY is the clean loser with USD/JPY at ~163.2.
meta.nextBigEvent: The ECB decision (Thu Jul 23, 12:15Z) plus Australia jobs (Jul 23) open four central-bank binaries in nine days (ECB Jul 23, FOMC Jul 28-29 now LIVE at ~46.5%, BoE Jul 30, BoJ Jul 31), with the failed-ceasefire oil re-fire running as the dominant risk over all of it.

dailyRead: Yesterday's two-way resolved to the upside and the whole oil-premium map snapped back on. The 10-day ceasefire was not accepted, Iran hit a second tanker in the Strait of Hormuz overnight, and the Houthis threatened a naval blockade of Saudi Arabia - so crude that stalled on Monday re-fired hard: WTI ~$84.3 (+2.2%), Brent ~$91.1 (+2.1%). That restores every leg the ceasefire optionality had softened: CAD's terms-of-trade tailwind is back, the USD's oil-inflation bid is back - and it now has a hard number behind it: CME July-hike odds jumped to ~46.5% (from 34% Sunday) with the Jul 28-29 FOMC now a near coin-flip - and gold ripped to ~$4,115 with silver +4.5% to ~$59.7. The day's fresh catalyst is British: UK June CPI ran HOT at 3.6% y/y (vs 3.4% exp, up from 3.4%) with core at 3.7% (vs 3.5%), motor fuels the biggest upward contributor - the exact fuel-pass-through mechanism that lit NZD yesterday, now lifting GBP and hardening the BoE's two hike dissents into Jul 30. Net: GBP is today's mover, the hawkish cluster (USD/CAD/GBP/NZD) broadens, and JPY is the clean loser - USD/JPY punched to ~163.2, deeper above the MoF line at a fresh 40-year low. Cleanest leans: AUD/NZD SHORT (HIGH) still the purest catalyst-asymmetry trade; the JPY carry-longs (CAD/JPY, NZD/JPY, GBP/JPY LONG, MED) back with oil; USOIL / XAU / XAG upgraded to LONG; NZD/CAD flips back to RANGE as CAD's oil leg reheats. The standing landmine is now flashing: spot 163.2 is deep in the strike zone and the ECB (Jul 23) opens three CB binaries in nine days.

today.headline: UK June CPI ran HOT at 3.6% y/y (vs 3.4% exp, up from 3.4%) with core 3.7% (vs 3.5%) - motor fuels the biggest contributor.
today.read: GBP is the day's mover: the fuel-driven beat re-arms the BoE hawks and hardens both hike dissents into Jul 30 (BoE + MPR). It is the same oil pass-through that lit NZD - powerful but narrow, so watch for the MPC signalling it looks through fuel.

today.headline: The ceasefire failed and crude re-fired to WTI ~$84.3 / Brent ~$91.1, restoring CAD's terms-of-trade tailwind.
today.read: CAD reclaims its oil crown after briefly slipping on the ceasefire optionality. The hawkish leg is oil, not data - June core stays soft (trim 1.8%) - so AUD/CAD re-flips to SHORT while NZD/CAD flips back to RANGE.

today.headline: USD/JPY punched to ~163.2 - deeper above the MoF line at a fresh 40-year low - as re-firing oil hit the energy importer hardest.
today.read: Still the clean loser, but the offset builds: Tokyo trend CPI at 1.9% hands the BoJ ammunition at Jul 31. Spot is now deep in the strike zone - size every XXX/JPY carry-long for a violent MoF snap.

today.headline: CME July-hike odds JUMPED to ~46.5% (from 34% Sunday) as re-firing oil put the dollar's inflation bid back with a number.
today.read: The Jul 28-29 FOMC is now a live meeting, not a formality. DXY firms broadly but stays mid-pack, losing to hawkish-CPI GBP and hot-CPI NZD; a ceasefire would deflate the whole scare.

today.headline: With crude back up, the September-hike case hardens - a Reuters poll now shows ~70% of economists expecting a second 2026 ECB hike.
today.read: Firmer into tomorrow's ECB (Jul 23), which is a hold (<5% hike priced). Lagarde's tone and any September signal is the entire EUR trade this week; a ceasefire removes the energy argument.

today.headline: No change - the hot 4.1% Q2 CPI holds, and re-firing oil now renews the petrol driver rather than unwinding it.
today.read: Back-to-back RBNZ hikes into Aug 19 stay live and the ceasefire risk to the fuel component has receded. Still narrow (ex-fuel 2.9%) - the AUD/NZD short rides the catalyst asymmetry vs an AUD with jobs a risk tomorrow.

today.headline: No change - unemployment at 4.4% and loosening, with June jobs Jul 23 and Q2 CPI not due until Jul 29.
today.read: AUD is the passenger while GBP and NZD get hawkish fuel-CPI prints. Jobs tomorrow are a two-way risk; a further unemployment rise kills the thin Aug 11 RBA hike tail (~35%).

strength.drivers: June CPI 3.6% y/y (vs 3.4% exp, up from 3.4%) with core 3.7% (vs 3.5%), motor fuels the top contributor; unemployment 4.6%, DMP wages 4.2%; BoE 3.75% on a hawkish 7-2 hold with two hike votes.
strength.forward: 🦅 June CPI at 3.6% with core 3.7% overshot expectations, motor fuels the top contributor - the same oil pass-through that lit NZD, now under GBP. It hardens both hike dissents and the Bank's own path (CPI past 3.25% into Q4) into Jul 30 (BoE + MPR). Risk: the beat is fuel-led and the MPC looks through it; gilt jitters cap the upside; a risk-off flush hits high-beta GBP.

strength.drivers: Core CPI 2.6% (Jun, flat m/m), headline 3.5%; NFP +57k with unemployment 4.2%; Fed 3.50-3.75% on a hawkish hold; CME July-hike odds ~46.5% (from 34% Sunday), Sept ~50-55%; DXY ~100.7.
strength.forward: 🦅 Crude's re-fire put the dollar's inflation bid back and it now has teeth: CME July-hike odds jumped to ~46.5% (from 34% Sunday), making the Jul 28-29 FOMC a genuine live meeting rather than a formality; Sept ~50-55%. Warsh's 'prices are too high' plus a re-spiking oil bill is the hawkish engine. Risk: a ceasefire deflates the whole scare; a soft Aug 12 CPI unwinds it.

strength.drivers: Q2 CPI 4.1% y/y (vs 4.0% exp) and 1.5% q/q, a two-year high above the RBNZ's own 3.9% call; OCR 2.50% after the Jul 8 hike; unemployment 5.3%; sectoral factor model 2.7%.
strength.forward: 🦅 The 4.1%/1.5% double beat keeps back-to-back hikes live into Aug 19; crucially, yesterday's ceasefire risk to the fuel component has receded as oil re-fired - the petrol +27.5% driver is being renewed, not unwound. Still narrow (ex-fuel 2.9%, sectoral model 2.7%). Risk: RBNZ officials explicitly discount fuel; a late ceasefire; a risk-off flush hits high-beta NZD.

strength.drivers: June CPI 2.8% (from 3.2%) with CPI-trim 1.8%, median 1.9%, common 2.6% - all cooled; BoC held 2.25% on Jul 15 for a sixth straight time; jobs +18.2k, unemployment 6.5%; WTI back to ~$84.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ The ceasefire failure and crude's jump back to WTI $84 / Brent $91 reinstate the terms-of-trade premium that briefly slipped - CAD is once again the board's clearest oil beneficiary. The offset is domestic: June CPI cooled to 2.8% with all three BoC core gauges falling (trim 1.8%), so the hawkish leg is oil, not data. Risk: a genuine ceasefire removes the premium outright; soft core caps the upside at the next BoC (Sep).

strength.drivers: June HICP 2.8% (from 3.2%) with core at 2.5%; unemployment 6.3%; ECB deposit rate 2.25% after the Jun 11 hike, meeting-by-meeting guidance; Jul 23 hold <5% hike priced; ~70% of economists see a Sept hike.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 With crude back up, the argument for a second 2026 hike in September strengthens - a Reuters poll now shows ~70% of economists expecting it, and ING/Deutsche/Goldman are aligned; the energy resurgence is the catalyst. Jul 23 ECB is a hold; Lagarde's tone and any Sept signal is the entire EUR trade. Risk: HICP back near target lets Lagarde lean neutral; a ceasefire removes the energy argument.

strength.drivers: Trimmed mean 3.6% (May) with headline 4.0% above the 2-3% band; unemployment 4.4% (May) and drifting higher; June jobs due Jul 23; RBA held 4.35% in June after 75bp of 2026 hikes.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Sticky core floors it, but unemployment at 4.4% and no domestic catalyst until Q2 CPI on Jul 29 leave AUD the passenger while GBP and NZD get hawkish prints. June jobs land Jul 23 - a further rise kills the thin Aug 11 hike tail (~35%). Risk: a hot Q2 CPI Jul 29 flips it fast; a ceasefire risk-on melt-up lifts high-betas.

strength.drivers: Tokyo core 1.6% (Jun) with the fuel-stripped trend gauge at 1.9%; headline 1.5% below the 2% target; unemployment 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26; BoJ 1.00% after the Jun 16 hike; USD/JPY ~163.2.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Crude's re-fire hits the energy-importing yen hardest and USD/JPY punched through to ~163.2 - deeper above the MoF line at a fresh 40-year low (verbal only so far; the record 11.73tn yen Apr/May intervention failed to hold within six weeks). The offset is real - Tokyo trend CPI at 1.9% hands the BoJ ammunition at Jul 31 - but spot is now firmly in the strike zone. Risk: an MoF strike snaps the yen higher in minutes; a hawkish BoJ Jul 31 breaks the carry.

catalysts.event: Ceasefire failure - 2nd Hormuz tanker hit; Houthis threaten Saudi blockade
catalysts.note: The single biggest two-way risk: the proposal was not accepted and escalation resumed, re-firing oil and every oil-linked leg. A late acceptance would unwind it all in a session.

catalysts.event: US strikes - 10th-plus consecutive night; Hormuz traffic sharply reduced
catalysts.note: The US naval blockade of Iranian ports is in force; every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY and gold.
Deep above the flagged line at a fresh 40-year low; verbal warnings only so far; the record 11.73tn yen Apr/May precedent failed to hold within six weeks.

geopolitics: The ceasefire failed and the oil trade is one-way up again. The 10-day proposal was not accepted; overnight Iran hit a second tanker in the Strait of Hormuz and the Houthis threatened a naval blockade of Saudi Arabia, on top of the US carrying out a 10th-plus consecutive night of strikes and a standing naval blockade of Iranian ports. Shipping through the strait - normally ~20% of world oil flow - is severely disrupted, and crude that stalled Monday re-fired: WTI ~$84.3 (+2.2%), Brent ~$91.1 (+2.1%), with US pump prices holding near $4/gallon. Yesterday's two-way has collapsed back to a one-sided premium, and the marginal headline risk is up until a deal is actually signed. FX transmission: (1) CAD reclaims its tailwind - the terms-of-trade premium that briefly slipped is restored with crude back at WTI $84 / Brent $91, though June core (trim 1.8%) keeps the hawkish leg oil-driven, not data-driven; AUD/CAD re-flips to SHORT and NZD/CAD flips back to RANGE. (2) The USD's oil-inflation bid is back with a number - CME July-hike odds jumped to ~46.5% (from 34% Sunday), turning the Jul 28-29 FOMC into a live meeting; DXY firms but stays mid-pack, losing to hawkish-CPI GBP and NZD. (3) The JPY is the clean loser - re-firing oil hits the energy importer hardest and USD/JPY punched to ~163.2, deep above the MoF line at a fresh 40-year low, even as Tokyo trend CPI at 1.9% builds the BoJ's hawkish case for Jul 31. (4) GBP joins the fuel-CPI hawks: UK June CPI at 3.6% (core 3.7%) was driven by motor fuels - the same mechanism as NZD - hardening the BoE dissents into Jul 30. (5) The EUR's September-hike case hardens as the energy resurgence renews the inflation argument into tomorrow's ECB. (6) Gold ripped to ~$4,115 and silver +4.5% to ~$59.7 as the haven bid returned in force. Every leg of today's map keys off the same binary - but today it broke the hawkish, oil-up way.
